economic_hypothesis: Quarter-hour opening imbalance tends to reverse over the first 15-30 minutes but its public-signal component is strongest at the 12-hour horizon. Waiting for the causal 30-minute retracement should improve a single-entry 12-hour continuation trade without paying for a reversal leg.

source_prior.paper: Kim and Hansen (2026), The Quarter-Hour Effect, arXiv:2607.09426v2
locked_facts: quarter-hour CFE is negative over the first half hour
public-component interquartile effect rises from 0.9 bp at 4h to 9.8 bp at 8h and 16.9 bp at 12h
12h public contribution share is the largest component for BTC, ETH, SOL and the cross-asset mean

information_availability.entry: marketable execution at open[T+32m] with adverse slippage
information_availability.capacity_reference: quote volume of minute [T+30m,T+31m), completed before entry
information_availability.exit: the original forecast endpoint open[T+12h+1m], leaving approximately 11h29m after entry
information_availability.public_features: same frozen T-15m information gap as V0.27.1
information_availability.monthly_refit: trailing 180 days strictly before test month; all labels end before month start; 12h purge

signal.funding_boundaries: opening boundaries at 00:00, 08:00 and 16:00 UTC excluded

execution.cost_robustness: the exact base-admitted signal set is replayed under stress and extreme costs
execution.stop: pre-boundary ATR multiple, tick rounded away from entry; liquidation and gap-stop logic identical to V0.27.1
execution.funding: official rate and mark while position exists
